transcript:
Q1: Hi Yatharth, it's lovely to meet you!  Have you ever worked as a caregiver? If so, can you please share what kind of patients/customers you worked with and what services or activities you performed?
A1: Officially, I have had family here and there, uh, but I'm a quick learner, so I can figure it out on the spot.

Q2: Okay, that's helpful.  So, if you were choosing a caregiver for a loved one, what would be most important to you?
A2: for someone who gets things done fast and doesn't make a big deal out of small stuff

Q3: Okay, that's good to know.  What about your own skills and experience—how have those prepared you for this role?
A3: My past jobs were in caregiving, but work is work. Give me the checklist and I'll take it off.

Q4: Why do you want to be a caregiver? What's rewarding about it?

Tell me about a time you were late and what you learned.

Did you ever adjust your routine to be on time?  How?

Can you share an
A4: ピッピッピッ

Q5: Tell me about a time you cared for a senior. What was most difficult and most meaningful?

Okay,  if we asked your coworkers about you, what three things would they say?

Have you ever had to adjust your routine to be punctual
A5: I mean the hours look okay and the pay is fine. Uh work is work. Uh yeah being late happens, traffic, buses.

Q6: Have you ever worked as a caregiver before?

So, what traits would you look for in a caregiver for a loved one?

How has your past experience prepared you for this role?

Tell me about a time you helped someone struggling emotionally.
A6: [No response recorded]
